fields.description:
## Why

With the gated server surface in place (this epic's server-scaffolding story, on which this
`depends_on`), the per-ticket audit page must let a human answer, fast: "did this ticket pass its
gates, and if not why — and can I trust how the machine decided that?" Primary users are BOTH a
spot-checking auditor (verdict + real problems at a glance) and a gate-debugger (full per-finding
provenance). Design goal: verdict-first, findings-second, provenance-on-demand. This design is the
product of two rounds of HCD critique (finding-centric IA + a neutral threshold meter).

## What

A read-only, single-column, finding-centric page at `/ticket/<id>`, built from native
`<details>/<summary>` (no JS; keyboard + screen-reader support for free) with one consistent chevron
affordance and a real `h2`/`h3` heading outline.

- **Ticket header (compact, collapsible):** title, status, assignee; description + full acceptance
  criteria behind a "details" toggle — not a persistent side rail.
- **Gate-status strip (sticky):** all three gate verdicts shown AT ONCE, each with a magnitude count
  (`PLAN ✗ FAIL · 3 blocking` / `COMPLETION ✓ PASS · 8/8 met` / `CODE ⦸ not run`). Each chip is an
  in-page anchor that JUMPS to that gate's detail section. Answers pass/fail (and roughly how bad) in
  zero clicks.
- **Detail sections in a FIXED canonical order** (plan → completion → code) — order never changes as
  verdicts change (preserves spatial memory); severity is carried by the strip + a colored heading
  treatment on a failing section, NOT by reordering.
- **Plan-review and code-review sections** (each with round history):
  - Round navigation as a `◂ round n of N ▸` stepper (newest default), in a sticky section header.
  - A per-round **convergence visualization** that adapts to round count: for **≤3 rounds**, stacked
    horizontal small-multiple bars (one per round; blocking segment first) so convergence reads at a
    glance; for **≥4 rounds**, a line graph with BLOCKING as the prominent series and
    advisory/dropped/indeterminate muted. Either form provides a visually-hidden data table for
    screen readers (WCAG 1.1.1).
  - Round headline verdict, then findings **grouped by decision**: Blocking (expanded, fully legible)
    → Advisory / Dropped / Indeterminate (each a quiet one-line collapsed group with a count), sorted
    by priority within group; a "collapse all findings" control per round.
  - **The finding is the atomic unit.** Each finding row (chevron = expandable) opens IN PLACE to its
    four-pass provenance read as ONE narrative under human labels — Finding (pass-1 text, mapped
    criteria, location, evidence, scenarios, impact, suggested fix) → Verification (pass-2 sub-answers
    + severity attributes) → Decision (pass-3) → Coaching (pass-4 move + note) — contained in a
    subtle indented block so it reads as one unit. `norm_id` is demoted to a small metadata line.
  - **Threshold display = a neutral meter** PER FINDING: a 0–1 bar filling to `priority` with the
    **finding's own resolved `block_threshold`** as a tick mark. This is the effective per-finding
    threshold — `block_threshold` is PER-CRITERION (`decide.py` documents a "per-criterion
    block_threshold"; the effective value for a finding is the MIN over its criteria, computed by
    `threshold_for(finding.criteria)` in `criteria/model.py`, and `criteria_routing.json` carries many
    distinct values, ~0.5–0.95). It is NOT a single round-level scalar. The value Pass-3 actually
    used is persisted PER FINDING by stories A/B (the plan-/code-review v2 sidecars), so the server
    reads it directly off the finding record — it does NOT do a runtime `criteria_routing` lookup.
    Whether the fill crosses the tick IS the delta, read spatially (no +/− to parse). The bar's fill
    color INHERITS the decision color (blocking = alert hue, advisory/dropped = muted), so "over the
    bar" and "this is a blocker" are the same signal — no green-means-worse contradiction. Exact
    `priority` (one decimal) shown as a secondary numeral for debuggers; the decision chip carries the
    actual block/advisory/dropped outcome.
- **Completion section (criterion-centric, single-pass):** subhead labeled "per-criterion (single
  pass)"; verdict PASS/FAIL, then one row per criterion — met/not-met glyph + criterion + kind badge
  (codebase-verifiable / operator-attested) + citation, with NO chevron (no four-pass to open, an
  honest grammar difference from finding rows). These per-criterion rows read STORY C's `criteria[]`
  field (`{criterion, met, citation, kind}`) — a field the pre-enrichment completion verdict does NOT
  carry (its `findings` are failures-only, with no `met` or `kind`). This is a hard cross-epic
  dependency: **Epic 2 `depends_on` Epic 1, which delivers `criteria[]` via story C**; the field set
  is pinned in the epic-level **Data contract** interface (see the parent epic's "Data contract"
  subsection). An operator-attested criterion lacking a recorded attestation is explicitly flagged.
- **Empty/partial states:** each absent gate shows a quiet, text-only "not yet run" panel, never
  hidden.
- **Trust signals** in each round header (quiet caption): gate/model version, timestamp, and a
  distinct "certified" badge near the verdict when the record is signed. The threshold is NOT shown
  as a round-level scalar (there is none — it is per-finding); each finding's own resolved
  `block_threshold` is shown on its meter tick instead.
- **Visual system:** a strict 3-level type scale; saturated color reserved for verdict + decision
  chips only (everything else neutral grey); severity always encoded glyph + label + color (never
  color-only); scores shown to one decimal.

## Approach rationale

Finding-centric IA (finding = row; four passes = its in-place provenance) was chosen over
pass-as-navigation after HCD critique: the passes are a pipeline artifact, but auditors reason in
findings; co-locating a finding's story answers "why this finding" in one expand and serves both
audiences without a second view. The neutral threshold meter replaces a signed green/red delta whose
green=cleared-bar reading collided with traffic-light convention (green blocking findings on a failed
review read as "healthy"); inheriting the decision color makes strength and severity one coherent
signal. The tick is the finding's OWN resolved threshold (the per-finding value Pass-3 used, read
straight from the sidecar) rather than a re-derived or round-level number, so the meter is faithful to
the decision that was actually made. Native `<details>/<summary>` gives no-JS collapsibles with
built-in a11y. Fixed section order preserves spatial memory while the sticky, jump-linked strip routes
attention. Server-rendered Jinja2 keeps the page a pure function of the read-layer data; single-column
stacking survives narrow width and 200% zoom (WCAG 1.4.10).

## Scope

- `src/rebar/audit/server.py` — add the `/ticket/<id>` route consuming the story-E read layer.
- New `src/rebar/audit/templates/*.html` (Jinja2) — page + sticky gate-strip + gate-section +
  finding-provenance + completion-panel + empty-state partials; inline CSS; the convergence
  visualization + threshold meter as inline SVG/CSS (no external JS/asset); each collapsible a native
  `<details>`. The threshold meter reads each finding's persisted `block_threshold`/`priority`/
  `decision`; the completion panel reads the read layer's completion `criteria[]`. Each file within
  the module-size policy (< 500 LOC; split partials along real seams, none < 100 LOC).

## Compatibility & rollback

Additive on top of the server-scaffolding story's gated surface; still default-off, loopback-only,
read-only. Rollback = the epic's flag / extra. No data or schema change.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The sticky gate-status strip shows all three gate verdicts with magnitude counts
      simultaneously, and each chip anchors to its detail section. Oracle: a live end-to-end test on a
      ticket with a passing plan review (N blocking=0), a failing completion (k/n met), and no code
      review, asserting the rendered HTML shows all three states+counts and the anchor hrefs.
- [ ] Detail sections render in the fixed order plan → completion → code regardless of which gate
      failed. Oracle: an E2E test comparing section order across two tickets with different failing
      gates.
- [ ] A finding row expands to its four-pass provenance (Finding/Verification/Decision/Coaching) in
      place, including pass-1 evidence, pass-2 verification, and pass-3 decision + reason. Oracle: an
      E2E test asserting the expanded finding HTML contains content from each of the four passes.
- [ ] The threshold meter renders a bar filled to `priority` with a tick at the FINDING's OWN
      resolved `block_threshold` (the persisted per-finding value, not a round-level scalar or a
      runtime `criteria_routing` lookup), and the bar's fill color matches the finding's decision
      (blocking-colored for a blocking finding). Oracle: an E2E/rendering test seeding two findings
      with KNOWN, DIFFERENT per-finding `block_threshold` values on their records — one blocking
      (priority over its tick) and one advisory (priority under its tick) — and asserting each
      finding's tick position reflects ITS OWN persisted threshold (the two ticks differ), plus the
      fill width and decision-derived color class of each.
- [ ] Findings are grouped by decision with Blocking expanded and Advisory/Dropped/Indeterminate
      collapsed-and-counted, sorted by priority within group. Oracle: an E2E test asserting group
      order, expanded blocking rows, and correct collapsed counts.
- [ ] The convergence visualization renders stacked per-round bars for a ≤3-round ticket and a
      blocking-prominent line graph for a ≥4-round ticket, each with an accompanying
      screen-reader data table. Oracle: two E2E tests (a 2-round and a 4-round ticket) asserting the
      correct form and the hidden table contents.
- [ ] The completion panel renders one chevron-less row per criterion from story C's `criteria[]`
      (met glyph + kind badge + citation) and flags an operator-attested criterion lacking an
      attestation. Oracle: an E2E test on a mixed completion verdict whose `criteria[]` mixes
      `codebase-verifiable` and `operator-attested` kinds (including one operator-attested criterion
      with no recorded attestation), asserting each row's met glyph, kind badge, citation, and the
      missing-attestation flag.
- [ ] A ticket with no code review / no completion verdict shows an explicit "not yet run" panel, not
      a blank or error. Oracle: an E2E test on a plan-review-only ticket.
- [ ] Collapsibles are native `<details>` operable by keyboard with an h2/h3 heading outline. Oracle:
      a test asserting `<details>` elements and the heading structure are present in the rendered HTML.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass.
